summary: Heavily obfuscated shim warrants manual review
details: The package ships shim/index.js with heavy identifier obfuscation (hex-mangled variable names such as _0x391f3f, _0x3eff0a, _0x534564 on line 3), a pattern characteristic of javascript-obfuscator output. The behavior of the obfuscated code has not been traced to a concrete installer-harm mechanism (no confirmed exfiltration endpoint, no install-time fetch-and-execute, no credential read path). Obfuscation alone is not proof of malicious intent — some packages obfuscate for anti-tampering or anti-piracy reasons — but it materially reduces transparency and prevents automated verification of the runtime behavior. Recommend human review of the deobfuscated shim before trusting this version.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
